library_preparation_preflight.summary: The artifact is a Spring Boot auto-configuration module whose runtime dependencies are declared optional, so the normal scaffold would only get the target JAR and not the Spring AI retry or Spring Boot auto-configuration APIs needed for meaningful context/bean coverage.

deterministic_setup.reason: SpringAiRetryAutoConfiguration is conditional on org.springframework.ai.retry.RetryUtils and creates RetryTemplate/ResponseErrorHandler behavior using Spring AI retry exceptions and utilities; this dependency is optional in the target POM and is not pulled transitively.

The target classes use Spring Boot auto-configuration and configuration-properties annotations; this dependency is optional in the target POM and is required to load the auto-configuration meaningfully in a Spring context.

library_preparation_preflight.agent_guidance: No Docker image or external service is needed. Tests should exercise the auto-configuration by loading a Spring context and verifying the RetryTemplate and ResponseErrorHandler beans plus spring.ai.retry.* property binding. If executing retry behavior rather than only checking bean creation/error handling, configure very small retry/backoff values in the test context, for example max attempts of 2 and an initial interval around 1ms, to avoid slow tests.
risks: The artifact is tied to Spring Boot 4.1.0-RC1 and Spring Framework 7.0.x; dependency version drift could cause classpath conflicts.
Using only direct class instantiation would miss the auto-configuration and configuration-properties behavior that this artifact primarily provides.
